description_long: This object is a compact, military-style handheld radio transceiver with a robust, rectangular body and a long, black antenna extending from the top. The front face features a numeric keypad, a small display screen, and several labeled buttons, all set into a green and gray casing. The top of the device has two rotary knobs, likely for volume and channel selection, as well as a speaker grille. The sides of the radio show additional buttons and ports, possibly for push-to-talk functionality and accessory connections. The back of the device is relatively plain, with a visible battery compartment and a circular label or sticker. The overall design is utilitarian, with ruggedized surfaces and reinforced edges, suitable for outdoor or tactical urban use.
description: A rugged, green handheld radio transceiver with a long black antenna, numeric keypad, display, and rotary knobs, designed for field or tactical use.
description_view_0: Side view showing a slim profile with a side button and port, antenna extending upward.
description_view_1: Front view displaying the keypad, display screen, and control knobs, with the antenna centered above.
description_view_2: Opposite side view with a different button and port arrangement, antenna visible.
description_view_3: Rear view showing the battery compartment and a circular label, with the antenna at the top.
